Client newsletter is that he's produced for his clients that are in there so you can see it and check it out. So let's move on to the stealth way. A stealth way to get clients to refer even when they don't want to. So what is this stealth way and how does it work? Well, don't want to might be a bit strong, but feel uncomfortable or awkward doing it. So the chiropractor has a patient. So first of all, the patient came to the chiropractor because of back pain from an auto injury. So the frame of reference, the way Betty the patient thinks of Dave the doctor is has somebody who fixes backs after auto injuries. So her natural referral activity is going to be limited to a friend, family member, a co-worker who has a car wreck and is then complaining about back pain. So that's like our problem number one. Problem number two is it's still somewhat, it's more aggressive for most than most people are really organically comfortable. Hey Dave, you're complaining about back pain all the time. You should really go see my chiropractor. Let me tell you about my chiropractor, etc. So the... That improves with what we talked about in session two. All of that improves. Right, right. With the shock of the WAP package. But what gives them a better opportunity, a better way of doing things is to be referring to information rather than referring to a provider. Oh, so referring to the information as opposed to directly referring to you. Yeah. So we tell people about books that we've read all the time. We tell people about TV shows we watched all the time. We tell people about a website they can go to all the time. Much more natural behavior for us. So we use a system that I call the special report of the month system, which is every month to have a different condition, problem, need, desire, specific report that is purely informational. So now the chiropractor, one month, might be all about headaches. So the first thing we've done is Betty, who was only thinking about you for car wrecks and back pain, now thinks about you. And she has a co-worker who always is complaining about headaches and has to take a day off every so often because of migraines and so forth. Then we've given Betty a much easier way to make that connection because she can now go to a co-worker and say there's this really cool free report, 42 things you can do to eliminate headaches. And here's where you can get it. I thought it would be useful. I thought you'd want to know about it. Oh, that's great. And so the referral to information is far more natural for people than the referral to the provider. It's also, which we talked about in the first segment, it is much more likely, which is an important part of the criteria of why people refer or don't refer. The person referring now, Betty, in our scenario, is much more likely to get warm and enthusiastic gratitude for being told about this information than being pitched on going to the chiropractor. Right? So the special report of the month allows you to substitute information to refer to rather than a person, a place, a business to refer to. Yeah, so it's a two-step process because what you, what it's like lead generation. Well, it's exactly what it is. Yeah, because what you're doing, you're really asking in the other scenario, which you should be doing, but the other scenario, you're asking your customer, client or patient to make a sale almost, right? Or get very close to it. Or get very close to making a sale. This one, you're just saying. And they're likely to get. Yeah, it's beautiful. They're like, so you know when you go to a store. Right. All the dumb stores run by dumb people with dumb clerks in them have the clerk come up to you and say, can I help you? And 90% of the people say no, even though they might need help. Just looking, right. And they might want help. They say, no, I'm just looking. Right. I just did it myself the other day. I mean, it's like, you know, and afterwards I'm thinking. Wait a minute. Why did I do this? I just need some help. So I'm in this toy store, which I went to go get pirate stuff for the wealth academy. So I walk in and the guy owns a little toy store says, can I help you? I said, no, I'm just going to browse. Now I'm there for a specific purpose. The one reason I'm there is to find some pirate stuff. Why don't I say, got any pirate stuff and shortcut this process? But we like do it automatically. Right. So somebody starts to pitch us. So Betty starts to tell you about her chiropractor because I
